nodes.en: Protocols and procedures are documented sequences of steps for conducting operations, experiments, diplomatic exchanges, or medical treatments. They standardize methods and ensure reproducibility across practitioners and contexts.
nodes.cs: Protokoly a procedury jsou dokumentované posloupnosti kroků pro provádění operací, experimentů, diplomatických výměn nebo lékařských postupů. Standardizují metody a zajišťují reprodukovatelnost u praktikujících a v různých kontextech.
nodes.brief:
Protocols and procedures are documented, ordered sequences of steps for carrying out a repeatable activity — a laboratory experiment, a surgical intervention, a diplomatic exchange, an emergency response — written so that any qualified practitioner produces the same outcome. Where a formula supplies a value and a standard sets a requirement, a procedure specifies the sequence of actions that gets a practitioner from starting conditions to a defined result.

This node is broader in scope than [[09.03]] Technical Manuals, which document how to operate or repair one specific system: a manual is procedure bound to a product, while protocols here cover process-driven fields — clinical medicine, scientific method, diplomacy, safety response — that are not tied to any single artifact. [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications set the outcome a process must achieve; the procedure is the sequence that achieves it. The living-tradition counterpart is [[10.08]] Rituals & Ceremonies, which likewise sequences prescribed steps but transmits them through practice and observance rather than formal written documentation.

Rigor here means unambiguous, testable steps and version control, since a stale or ambiguous procedure directly causes reproducibility failure or harm. Clinical and research procedures typically reference the [World Health Organization](https://www.who.int/) for standardized health protocols, and diplomatic protocol traces to conventions such as the [Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations](https://legal.un.org/ilc/texts/instruments/english/conventions/9_1_1961.pdf).
